---
title: ""
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="70%"}

# Yuhan (Max) Wu

Undergraduate at **Emory University** (Class of 2028) studying Applied Mathematics & Statistics
and Economics. I work at the intersection of biostatistics, data science, and quantitative
analysis — with active research in cancer immunology and metabolic disease, and applied work
in public health analytics.

I'm building toward graduate study in biostatistics or computational biology, while staying
open to industry roles in insurance, pharma, consulting, and data analytics.

[yuhan.wu@emory.edu](mailto:yuhan.wu@emory.edu) · 
[LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/wuyuhanm) · 
[GitHub](https://github.com/UhanWu) ·
[Download CV](assets/YWu_CV.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-dark .btn-sm}

:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::


::::

---

## Education

**Emory University** — Atlanta, GA *(Aug 2024 – May 2028)*  
B.S. Applied Mathematics & Statistics · B.A. Economics  
GPA: 3.96/4.00 · SAT: 1540

**Relevant Coursework**

*Data Science & Computing:* Statistical Computing I (R), Statistical Computing II (Python),
Regression Analysis, Scientific Computing (Python), Intro to Computer Science (Java)

*Mathematics:* Mathematical Statistics, Linear Algebra, Multivariable Calculus, Numerical
Analysis (MATLAB), Differential Equations, Foundations of Mathematics (Proofs)

*Biomedical:* Foundations of Modern Biology, Chemistry: Structure and Properties

---

## Experience

### Research

**Undergraduate Research Assistant** · Winship Cancer Institute, Emory University *(Jan 2026 – Present)*  
Analyzing longitudinal survival and clinical trial data from 35 PDAC patients (77 samples) across
a Phase 1b immunotherapy trial. Integrated 71 cytokine/growth factor biomarkers with clinical
outcomes, profiled immune cell composition using CyTOF/mIHC, and conducted multi-omic analyses
linking CHIP mutations to immune suppression phenotypes. Co-first author paper in progress.

**Undergraduate Research Assistant** · Emory University School of Medicine *(Sep 2024 – Present)*  
Translational research in Dr. Fridovich-Keil's lab on classic galactosemia. Modeled longitudinal
and cross-sectional clinical data from 500+ patients in R using LMMs and MMRMs. Published in
*Journal of Inherited Metabolic Disease* (2026).

**Research Trainee (SIBDS)** · Columbia University Mailman School of Public Health *(May – Jul 2026)*  
Selected for NIH-funded Summer Institute in Biostatistics and Data Science. Conducted mentored
research in statistical modeling with reproducible workflows for biomedical data analysis.


### Industry & Applied

**Data Analyst** · Invest Atlanta *(Feb 2026 – May 2026)*  
Geospatial and demographic analysis evaluating fresh food access across Atlanta neighborhoods.
Developed gravity-weighted demand model to identify underserved areas and priority grant recipients.

**Medical Research Intern** · Berkeley Pharma Tech, Remote *(Aug 2025 – Jan 2026)*  
Led a 4-member cross-university team on neuromodulation treatments for treatment-resistant
depression. Authored core manuscript sections and directed submission per AMA citation standards.

**AI Trainer** · Handshake AI, Remote *(Jul – Aug 2025)*  
Designed and stress-tested domain-specific prompts to benchmark LLM performance in biomedical
research tasks. Assessed model outputs for scientific accuracy and technical depth.



---

## Publications & Presentations

Garrett, O. S., Smith, N. H., Cutler, D. J., **Wu, Y.**, … & Fridovich-Keil, J. L. (2026).
Heritability of long-term complications in classic galactosemia. *Journal of Inherited Metabolic
Disease*, 49(3), e70181. [doi:10.1002/jimd.70181](https://doi.org/10.1002/jimd.70181)

- Prenatal paper (>5 authorship) with Fridovich-Keil Lab — *in preparation*
- PDAC immunotherapy trial paper, Woo Lab — *co-first author, in preparation*
- Presented at the **2023 Joint Mathematics Meeting**, Boston, MA

---

## Awards & Honors

| Award | Year |
|---|---|
| SOA Exam P (Probability) — Passed | Sep 2025 |
| SOA Exam FM (Financial Mathematics) — In Preparation | 2026 |
| ASA DataFest @ Emory — Judges' Choice Award | 2026 |
| HiMCM Finalist (Top 63/967), COMAP | 2023 |
| HiMCM Meritorious, COMAP | 2021 |
| IMMC USA Regional Finalist, COMAP | 2022, 2024 |
| Modeling the Future Challenge — NE Regional #1, National Finalist | 2023, 2024 |

---

## Skills

**Programming:** R (5 yrs) · Python (4 yrs) · MATLAB · SQL · Java · LaTeX

**Platforms:** Git/GitHub · VS Code · Jupyter · ArcGIS · Anaconda · MySQL

**Languages:** English (Bilingual) · Mandarin Chinese (Bilingual) · Spanish · German

**Interests:** Swimming · Astrophotography · Piano

---

## Organizations

- **Emory Economics Review** — Managing Editor
- **Emory Residential Hall Association** — Secretary
- **Newton North Math Modeling Club** — Co-founder
